attractions.name: American Airlines Center
attractions.description: The American Airlines Center is a premier multi-purpose arena located in the heart of Dallas's Victory Park neighborhood. Opened in 2001, it serves as the home for both the Dallas Mavericks (NBA) and the Dallas Stars (NHL). The venue is renowned for its distinctive brick architecture and serves as a central hub for major concerts and live entertainment in North Texas (Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Tuesday & Thursday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Event Days: Opens at 10:00 AM for evening events or 2 hours prior for early events (Official Site). Arena doors typically open 60-90 minutes before event start times (Official Site).
attractions.days_open: Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Event Days (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; children under two years old do not require a ticket for most events if sitting on an adult's lap (Official Site). Tickets for games and concerts are primarily sold through Ticketmaster (Official Site).
highlights: Home to the NBA's Dallas Mavericks and NHL's Dallas Stars (Wikipedia).
Features the Dirk Nowitzki statue at PNC Plaza (Wikipedia).
Architecturally unique Quonset hut-style roof design (Wikipedia).
Host of high-profile events including the NBA Finals, NHL All-Star Game, and UFC (Wikipedia).
State-of-the-art luxury suites and theater boxes (Dallas Sports Commission).
Environmentally friendly facility with multiple awards for green technology (Dallas Sports Commission).
attractions.tips: The arena is a strictly cashless venue; reverse ATMs are available on-site (Official Site). There is a firm bag policy: small clutches (4.5"x6.5") are allowed at all entries, while purses up to 14"x14"x6" must go through X-ray lanes; backpacks are prohibited (Official Site). Use mobile ticketing via the Ticketmaster or team apps for entry (Official Site).
attractions.nearby: Perot Museum of Nature and Science, Klyde Warren Park, Dallas World Aquarium, House of Blues Dallas (Tripadvisor, Dallas.com)

attractions.name: AT&T Discovery District
attractions.description: The AT&T Discovery District is an immersive downtown destination where technology, culture, and entertainment combine to create unique experiences. It features a massive 104-foot Media Wall, the iconic 'Spirit of Communication' statue, and a variety of dining and retail options integrated into AT&T’s global headquarters. (AT&T Discovery District)
attractions.region: Downtown Dallas / Main Street District (Downtown Dallas, Inc.)
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Thursday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Friday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Saturday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM; Sunday: 7:00 AM - 2:00 AM (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (AT&T Discovery District)
highlights: The Media Wall: A 104-ft-tall LED screen wrapping the building at Akard and Jackson Streets (AT&T Discovery District)
The Globe: A 30-foot mirror-finished sculpture with interactive lighting and sound (Electrosonic)
Spirit of Communication (Golden Boy): An iconic 24-foot bronze statue relocated to the heart of the district (Wikipedia)
Exchange Food Hall: A two-story culinary destination featuring 16+ unique dining stalls (AT&T Discovery District)
The Plaza: A family-friendly outdoor space with a lawn, seating, and free public Wi-Fi (AT&T Discovery District)
Interactive Tech Installations: Seamlessly integrated digital experiences powered by 5G and wind energy (Gensler)
attractions.tips: Self-parking is available at the 1212 Jackson Street garage and is complimentary with merchant validation from The Exchange or AT&T Store after 4 p.m. on weekdays and all day on weekends. Pets on a leash are welcome in The Plaza area, and visitors are encouraged to bring lawn chairs or blankets for movie nights. (AT&T Discovery District)
attractions.nearby: Reunion Tower, The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza, Giant Eyeball, John F. Kennedy Memorial Plaza, Dallas Arts District (Visit Dallas)

attractions.name: Bullion
attractions.description: Bullion is an upscale French brasserie located in the heart of downtown Dallas, featuring the culinary vision of Michelin-starred chef Bruno Davaillon. The restaurant is celebrated for its stunning mid-century modern design, including a striking gold-plated exterior and a dramatic spiral staircase entrance. Guests can enjoy a menu that masterfully blends traditional French techniques with contemporary flair, served in an elegant and luxurious atmosphere.
attractions.region: Downtown Dallas / Historic West End District
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Tuesday: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Wednesday: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Thursday: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Friday: 7:30 AM - 4:30 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Entrées: $15 - $36+, Lunch starters: $9 - $14, Special dishes up to $65+ (Free to enter; costs based on consumption)
highlights: Culinary leadership by Michelin-starred chef Bruno Davaillon
Striking architectural design with a gold-plated exterior and spiral staircase
Extensive wine list featuring French gems and high-end vintages
Sophisticated bar program with craft cocktails and a dedicated lounge menu
Traditional French specialties like Escargot Ravioli, Duck l'Orange, and Soufflés
Prime location within the renovated historic 400 Record building in downtown Dallas
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended due to high demand for dinner service. Arrive at least 30 minutes early to enjoy a craft cocktail at the bar and appreciate the restaurant's unique 'James Bond villain lair' architecture. The dress code is business casual or 'nice attire,' with many guests choosing to dress up for the occasion. Valet parking is available and recommended for ease of access in the downtown area.
attractions.nearby: Reunion Tower, John F. Kennedy Memorial Plaza, Dealey Plaza, Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum, Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza

attractions.name: Café Momentum
attractions.description: Café Momentum is an award-winning fine-dining restaurant and non-profit that transforms the lives of justice-involved youth by providing them with 12-month paid internships. Interns gain life skills, education, and employment opportunities while serving unique New American cuisine in a professional environment. (Cafe Momentum)
attractions.hours: Thursday: 5:30 PM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 5:30 PM – 9:30 PM, Saturday: 5:30 PM – 9:30 PM, Sunday-Wednesday: Closed (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant with a la carte menu; main dishes $18-$48) (Menu)
highlights: Award-winning 12-month paid internship program for justice-involved youth (Cafe Momentum)
Consistently named one of the top restaurants in Dallas for New American cuisine (Visit Dallas)
Offers comprehensive wraparound care including mental health support and life skills training (Cafe Momentum)
Founder Chad Houser honored as a CNN Hero for the restaurant's social impact (Cafe Momentum)
Flagship location serving as a national training center for future expansion (Cafe Momentum)
Interns achieve a significantly lower recidivism rate compared to state averages (YouTube)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended via OpenTable. Dining here directly supports their mission to empower youth. Try their signature smoked fried chicken, which is a fan favorite. (Cafe Momentum, Southern Living)
attractions.nearby: Thanks-Giving Square, Giant Eyeball, AT&T Discovery District, Majestic Theatre, Dallas Museum of Art, Klyde Warren Park (Tripadvisor, Evendo)

attractions.name: Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum Official Website
attractions.description: The Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum is dedicated to teaching the history of the Holocaust and advancing human rights to combat prejudice, hatred, and indifference. Located in Dallas's historic West End, the museum features three main wings—the Holocaust/Shoah Wing, the Human Rights Wing, and the Pivot to America Wing—as well as the Dimensions in Testimony Theater where visitors can interact with holographic images of Holocaust survivors. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 300 N. Houston Street, Dallas, TX 75202 Official Website, CityPASS
attractions.region: West End Historic District, Downtown Dallas Wikipedia, Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Thursday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Friday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Saturday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.; Sunday: 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m. Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Wednesday–Monday (Closed on Tuesdays) Official Website, CityPASS
attractions.admission: Adults: $19; Seniors (55+): $17; Military/First Responders: $17; Educators: $17; Visitors with Disabilities: $17; Students: $12; Members: Free Official Website
highlights: Holocaust / Shoah Wing exploring the history of the Holocaust Official Website
Human Rights Wing detailing the development of human rights Official Website
Pivot to America Wing focusing on the American experience of human rights Official Website
Dimensions in Testimony Theater for interactive holographic survivor testimony Official Website
Cinemark XD Theater and memorial area D Magazine
attractions.tips: Visitors should plan to spend two to three hours to experience the full museum. Photography is permitted but must be non-flash, and no video or tripods are allowed. Covered parking is available across the street at 301 N. Houston. It is recommended to book tickets online in advance to ensure entry. Official Website FAQ, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza, Old Red Museum of Dallas County History & Culture, John F. Kennedy Memorial Plaza, and various restaurants in the West End Historic District. Official Website FAQ, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Dallas Museum of Art (DMA)
attractions.description: The Dallas Museum of Art (DMA) is one of the ten largest art museums in the United States, located in the heart of the vibrant Dallas Arts District. Its encyclopedic collection contains over 24,000 works spanning 5,000 years of human creativity from around the globe. The museum is renowned for its innovative exhibitions, groundbreaking educational programs, and its commitment to providing free general admission to the public (Dallas Museum of Art, Wikipedia).
attractions.region: Arts District / Downtown Dallas (Yelp, Visit Dallas)
attractions.hours: Monday–Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday–Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Dallas Museum of Art)
attractions.admission: General Admission: Free; Special Exhibitions: Paid (typically $20 Adults, $18 Seniors/Military, $16 Students); DMA Members: Free (Dallas Museum of Art, Yelp)
highlights: The Keir Collection of Islamic Art (Visit Dallas)
Frederic Edwin Church’s "The Icebergs" (Best DFW Tours)
The Wendy and Emery Reves Collection (Wikipedia)
Jackson Pollock’s "Cathedral" (Best DFW Tours)
Claude Monet’s "Water Lilies" (Best DFW Tours)
Ancient American Gold and Textiles (Visit Dallas)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to reserve free general admission tickets online in advance. Underground parking is available for $20, or street parking can be found nearby. The museum is conveniently located across from Klyde Warren Park, making it easy to visit food trucks for lunch between galleries. Use the free M-Line Trolley for easy access to and from other downtown attractions (Dallas Museum of Art, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Klyde Warren Park, Nasher Sculpture Center, Crow Museum of Asian Art, Perot Museum of Nature and Science, Morton H. Meyerson Symphony Center (Tripadvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Dealey Plaza National Historic Landmark District
attractions.description: Dealey Plaza is a historic 3.1-acre city park in downtown Dallas, famously known as the site of the assassination of President John F. Kennedy on November 22, 1963. Designated a National Historic Landmark, the plaza is often called the "birthplace of Dallas" and features landmarks like the Grassy Knoll and the former Texas School Book Depository. Wikipedia, Dallas Parks
attractions.region: West End Historic District, Downtown Dallas
attractions.hours: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM Daily Waze, Dallas Parks Rule
attractions.admission: Free (Plaza & Grassy Knoll); Sixth Floor Museum requires paid tickets (Adults $27, Seniors $25, Youth $23, Children 0-5 Free) Visit Dallas, Viator
highlights: Grassy Knoll: A small sloping hill and infamous witness location. Wikipedia
Texas School Book Depository: The building from which the fatal shots were fired, now housing the Sixth Floor Museum. jfk.org
Triple Underpass: The railroad overpass where the presidential motorcade raced after the shooting. Wikipedia
X on Elm Street: A mark on the pavement indicating the spot where President Kennedy was fatally shot. Tripadvisor
George Dealey Statue: A bronze monument honoring the plaza's namesake and civic leader. Wikipedia
Bryan Pergola: A concrete structure honoring Dallas founder John Neely Bryan. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to book timed-entry tickets for the Sixth Floor Museum in advance. Parking is available in various paid lots nearby, but it can be difficult to find. Be respectful as the area is a somber site of a tragic historical event. jfk.org, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: The Sixth Floor Museum, JFK Memorial Plaza, Old Red Museum of Dallas County History & Culture, Dallas World Aquarium, Reunion Tower, Pioneer Plaza Visit Dallas, Reddit

attractions.name: Dee and Charles Wyly Theatre
attractions.description: The Dee and Charles Wyly Theatre is an iconic 12-story vertical structure in the Dallas Arts District, recognized for its innovative "stacked" design and aluminum-rod facade. It features the highly versatile Potter Rose Performance Hall, which can be reconfigured into various stage arrangements, from proscenium to thrust, to bring performers and audiences closer together. (AT&T PAC, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Tue-Fri: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sat: 1:00 PM – 6:00 PM; Sun-Mon: Closed (Box Office hours; venue opens 90 minutes before scheduled performances). (Dallas Theater Center)
attractions.admission: Varies by performance; typically ranges from $77 to $150+ per ticket. Check official site for current show pricing. (SeatGeek, Vivid Seats)
attractions.phone: 214-880-0202 (AT&T PAC) or 214-522-8499 (Dallas Theater Center) (AT&T PAC, Hall Arts Hotel)
highlights: Architectural landmark designed by Pritzker Prize winner Rem Koolhaas and Joshua Prince-Ramus. (Wikipedia)
Unique vertical design that "stacks" theater facilities to maximize the compact urban footprint. (Dallas Arts District)
State-of-the-art Potter Rose Performance Hall with flexible stage and seating configurations. (AT&T PAC)
Distinctive exterior features extruded anodized aluminum rods meant to resemble the folds of a theater curtain. (Wikipedia)
Home to world-class resident companies including the Dallas Theater Center and Dallas Black Dance Theatre. (Dallas Theater Center)
Located within the heart of the 68-acre Dallas Arts District, one of the largest in the U.S. (Dallas Arts District)
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes early to navigate congestion in the Arts District. Use the Lexus Silver underground parking for the most direct theater access. Note that foyer restrooms are designed to blend into the wall treatments and can be difficult to locate. (Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Winspear Opera House, Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center, Klyde Warren Park, Meyerson Symphony Center, Crow Museum of Asian Art. (Visit Dallas, Hall Arts Hotel)

attractions.name: Ellen's Southern Kitchen
attractions.description: Ellen's Southern Kitchen is a beloved Dallas staple specializing in scratch-made Southern comfort food served all day. Named after the owner's mother, the restaurant is famous for its hearty breakfast and brunch classics, including signature items like the Pancake Pot Pie and Shrimp & Grits. Official Website, CultureMap Dallas
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (Menu prices apply) Official Website
highlights: Scratch-made Southern comfort food served all day long.
Signature Pancake Pot Pie featuring layers of pancakes, maple cream sausage gravy, bacon, and eggs.
Unique "Joe\
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to avoid the long brunch crowds, or use the online 'Join the Waitlist' feature. Thursday is a great day to visit for half-price mimosas. Yelp, Official Website
attractions.nearby: The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza, John Neely Bryan Cabin, Old Red Museum of Dallas County History & Culture, Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Klyde Warren Park
attractions.description: Klyde Warren Park is a 5.2-acre deck park built over the recessed Woodall Rodgers Freeway, serving as a central gathering space that connects Downtown and Uptown Dallas. Since its opening in 2012, this urban oasis has provided a vibrant community hub with year-round free programming, including fitness classes, concerts, and educational activities (Klyde Warren Park Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM daily (Klyde Warren Park FAQ)
attractions.admission: Free (Klyde Warren Park FAQ)
highlights: Nancy Best Fountain: A massive interactive water feature with daily music and light shows (Official Site)
Sheila and Jody Grant Children’s Park: An 18,000-square-foot playground with climbing structures and interactive water features (Official Site)
My Best Friend's Park: A shady, fenced-in dog park where pets can run off-leash (Official Site)
Reading Room: A relaxing area with free books, magazines, and board games for public use (Official Site)
Food Truck Lane: A daily rotating selection of local food trucks offering a wide variety of cuisines (Official Site)
Muse Family Performance Pavilion: A central outdoor stage that hosts films, concerts, and fitness classes (Official Site)
attractions.tips: Free Wi-Fi is available park-wide courtesy of The Dallas Morning News; check the daily event schedule online for free programming like yoga or movies; pets are allowed off-leash in the designated dog park; and visitors should arrive early or use valet parking as street parking is limited and strictly enforced (Klyde Warren Park FAQ).
attractions.nearby: Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center, Perot Museum of Nature and Science, Morton H. Meyerson Symphony Center, Dallas Arts District (Dallas Arts District)

attractions.name: Margot and Bill Winspear Opera House
attractions.description: The Margot and Bill Winspear Opera House is a 21st-century reinterpretation of the traditional horseshoe-shaped opera house, engineered specifically for opera, musical theatre, and dance. Designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architect Norman Foster, the venue is renowned for its world-class acoustics and striking modern architecture within the Dallas Arts District. AT&T Performing Arts Center, The Dallas Opera
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Performance Days: Opens 90 minutes prior to curtain; General Building: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily (typical). AT&T Performing Arts Center, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Open daily (Box Office Monday-Friday and performance weekends; Lobby on performance days) AT&T Performing Arts Center, The Dallas Opera
attractions.admission: Prices vary by performance; Free Center tours available; student rush, military, and senior discounts for select shows. AT&T Performing Arts Center, The Dallas Opera
highlights: 2,200-seat Margaret McDermott Performance Hall with gold-leaf covered balconies. The Dallas Opera
Iconic Moody Foundation Chandelier featuring 300 LED rods that retract to music by Philip Glass. The Dallas Opera
Annette and Harold Simmons Signature Glass Facade reaching 60 feet high. AT&T Performing Arts Center
Three-acre slatted Sky Canopy providing shade and defining outdoor gathering spaces. AT&T Performing Arts Center
Vibrant red glass interior panels that create a seamless visual between indoor and outdoor spaces. Dallas Arts District
attractions.tips: Arrive 90 minutes early to enjoy the lobby and concessions; look up to see the chandelier retract before the show starts; Lexus drivers may receive complimentary parking. AT&T Performing Arts Center, The Dallas Opera
attractions.nearby: Meyerson Symphony Center, Wyly Theatre, Klyde Warren Park, Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center. The Dallas Opera, Reddit

attractions.name: Morton H. Meyerson Symphony Center Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Morton H. Meyerson Symphony Center is globally recognized as one of the world's greatest orchestra halls and is the only concert hall designed by Pritzker Prize-winning architect I.M. Pei. Opened in 1989, it serves as the permanent home of the Dallas Symphony Orchestra and features a modified 'shoebox' design with sophisticated adjustable acoustics Wikipedia, Dallas Observer
attractions.address: 2301 Flora Street, Dallas, TX 75201 Dallas Symphony Orchestra
attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 10AM – 6PM; Concert Days (Thursday – Saturday): 10AM – 8PM; Concert Days (Sunday): 12PM – 3:30PM Dallas Symphony Orchestra
attractions.days_open: Open daily for box office and events; however, Guest Services is closed on Saturdays and Sundays when no concert is scheduled Dallas Symphony Orchestra
attractions.admission: Ticket prices typically range from $44.10 to $161.10, with an average price of approximately $95.40. Free public tours and organ recitals are offered on select days TicketSmarter, The Flash List
highlights: Designed by world-renowned architect I.M. Pei and acoustician Russell Johnson Wikipedia
Home to the Eugene McDermott Concert Hall with its exceptional 'shoebox' acoustics Clio
Features the Lay Family Concert Organ, a massive instrument with 4,535 pipes The Flash List
Innovative acoustic system including a 40-ton canopy and reverberation chambers Clio
Houses significant art pieces, including Ellsworth Kelly's 'Dallas Panels' Dallas Observer
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes before performances; photography and recording are strictly prohibited during concerts. It is customary to refrain from clapping between movements of a musical piece. Parking is available at the Hall Arts Center and Cathedral garages Dallas Symphony Orchestra, The Flash List
attractions.nearby: Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center, Winspear Opera House, Klyde Warren Park, and Perot Museum of Nature and Science Dallas Arts District

attractions.name: Pioneer Plaza (Cattle Drive Sculpture)
attractions.description: Pioneer Plaza is a 4.2-acre public park in downtown Dallas featuring the world's largest bronze sculpture of its kind. The centerpiece is a monumental cattle drive recreation consisting of 49 larger-than-life longhorn steers and three cowboys on horseback. The site commemorates the historic Shawnee Trail that brought early settlers and trade to the region (Texas Trees Foundation, Visit Dallas).
attractions.region: Downtown Dallas / Convention Center District (Visit Dallas)
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: Sun-up to sun-down (Texas Trees Foundation)
attractions.admission: Free (Texas Trees Foundation, Experience First)
highlights: World's largest bronze sculpture of its kind (Visit Dallas)
49 bronze steers and three trail riders created by artist Robert Summers (CBS Texas)
Man-made stream, waterfall, and limestone cliffs reflecting natural Texas terrain (Texas Trees Foundation)
Commemorates the 19th-century Shawnee Trail cattle drives (Texas Trees Foundation)
Second most-visited landmark in downtown Dallas after Dealey Plaza (Texas Trees Foundation)
attractions.tips: The sculptures are best viewed during daylight hours for photography. Wear comfortable shoes as the park features uneven terrain and natural-style paths. Free parking is sometimes available nearby after 6:00 PM on weekdays and during weekends (Yelp, Experience First).
attractions.nearby: Kay Bailey Hutchison Convention Center, Pioneer Park Cemetery, Dallas City Hall, Dealey Plaza, The Sixth Floor Museum, John Neely Bryan Cabin (Visit Dallas, Expedia)

attractions.name: Reunion Tower
attractions.description: Reunion Tower is an iconic 561-foot observation tower in Dallas, famous for its distinctive geodesic dome known as "The Ball." From its GeO-Deck at 470 feet, visitors can enjoy breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views of the Dallas skyline and surrounding areas using both indoor and outdoor viewing platforms (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Mon: 1:30 PM - 9:30 PM, Tue: 4:30 PM - 9:30 PM, Wed: 4:30 PM - 9:30 PM, Thu: 1:30 PM - 9:30 PM, Fri: 1:30 PM - 9:30 PM, Sat: 12:00 PM - 10:00 PM, Sun: 1:30 PM - 9:30 PM (Ticket sales close 30 minutes prior to closing) (Official Website Calendar)
attractions.admission: Adults: $19 – $40, Seniors (65+): $16 – $35, Youth (4-12): $10 – $20, Children 3 and under: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 360-degree panoramic views of Dallas from 470 feet up (Visit Dallas)
Interactive 'Halo' touchscreens to explore landmarks and city history (Wikipedia)
Indoor and outdoor GeO-Deck observation areas with high-definition cameras (Official Website)
Signature geodesic dome featuring 259 programmable LED light fixtures (Wikipedia)
Fine dining at Crown Block restaurant specializing in steaks and seafood (D Magazine)
Free digital souvenir photo included with every general admission ticket (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance to secure your spot and potentially save on pricing. Visit during sunset to watch the city lights come alive as the skyline transitions from day to night. Be sure to check the 'Halo' interactive screens on the GeO-Deck to learn more about the specific buildings you are seeing in the distance (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Dealey Plaza, The Sixth Floor Museum, Hyatt Regency Dallas, John Fitzgerald Kennedy Memorial, Pioneer Plaza, and Dallas Union Station (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Thanks-Giving Square
attractions.description: Thanks-Giving Square is an interfaith complex and urban oasis in Downtown Dallas designed by architect Philip Johnson to inspire gratitude and unity (Dallas Insights). The site features a landscaped garden set below street level, a non-denominational spiral chapel with the iconic 'Glory Window' stained glass, and a museum dedicated to the tradition of thanksgiving (Wikipedia). It serves as a peaceful refuge and community gathering space, recognized as a major national shrine (Thanks-Giving Foundation).
attractions.hours: Garden: Monday-Sunday, 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Chapel: Monday-Sunday, 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Hall of Thanks-Giving: Open for special events or by appointment (Thanks-Giving Foundation)
attractions.admission: Free (Thanks-Giving Foundation)
highlights: Glory Window: A stunning spiral of 73 stained-glass panels designed by Gabriel Loire (Thanks-Giving Foundation).
Ring of Thanks: A 14-foot gold-leafed aluminum ring resting on the Circle of Giving (Wikipedia).
Bell Tower: Features three bronze bells modeled after the Liberty Bell (Dallas Insights).
Golden Rule Mosaic: A rendition of Norman Rockwell’s painting created by Venetian mosaicists (Thanks-Giving Foundation).
Wall of Presidents: A display celebrating the thanksgiving words of American presidents (Wikipedia).
The Garden: A serene space set 15 feet below ground level with waterfalls and channels to mask city noise (Texas State Historical Association).
attractions.tips: Self-guided tour guides are available in multiple languages on the official website; for a more immersive experience, look up through the Glory Window inside the chapel (Thanks-Giving Foundation). Accessible entry is available via a winding ramp at the corner of Ervay and Bryan streets (Dallas Insights). Public parking is available at the nearby garage at 1516 Pacific Avenue (Thanks-Giving Foundation).
attractions.nearby: Giant Eyeball, Majestic Theater, Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center, Klyde Warren Park, First Baptist Church, and Neiman Marcus (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: The Dallas World Aquarium
attractions.description: The Dallas World Aquarium is an immersive for-profit aquarium and zoo located in a converted warehouse in downtown Dallas. It features multi-level exhibits that simulate diverse global ecosystems, including the Orinoco Rainforest and Mundo Maya, housing a wide variety of endangered and exotic wildlife. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.region: West End Historic District / Downtown Dallas
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM (Last entry at 4:00 PM) Official Website
attractions.days_open: Daily (closed Thanksgiving and Christmas) Official Website
attractions.admission: Adult: $34.95 + tax, Child (3-12): $24.95 + tax, Child (2 and under): Free, Senior (65+): $28.95 + tax, Military: $28.95 + tax Official Website
highlights: Cloud Forest Trek featuring three-toed sloths and Andean animals
Mundo Maya exhibit with a 400,000-gallon shark tunnel walkthrough
Orinoco Rainforest housing monkeys, birds, and Antillean manatees
South Africa exhibit home to black-footed penguins and Madagascar wildlife
Borneo exhibit showcasing Australasian birds, fish, and reptiles
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds, especially on weekends; allow at least 2-3 hours for your visit; note that no outside food is permitted inside the facility. Tripadvisor, Official Website
attractions.nearby: Perot Museum of Nature and Science, The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza, Klyde Warren Park, Reunion Tower, Dallas Museum of Art, American Airlines Center Tripadvisor, Mapcarta

attractions.name: The Majestic Theatre
attractions.description: The Majestic Theatre is a historic performing arts venue in downtown Dallas, originally opened in 1921 as a vaudeville house. Designed by John Eberson, it later served as a premier movie palace hosting major film premieres before being restored and gifted to the City of Dallas. Today, it stands as the last remaining theater on Dallas's former 'Theater Row' and hosts a wide variety of concerts, comedy shows, and theatrical performances. Official Site
attractions.hours: Box Office: Open on show dates only, typically 3 hours prior to ticketed time. ATG Tickets
attractions.days_open: Open on event days; Box Office open 3 hours prior to showtime. ATG Tickets
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; typically $30 - $150+. No general admission for sightseeing. Ticketmaster
highlights: Last remaining theater on Dallas's historic 'Theater Row' TSHA
Renaissance Revival style architecture designed by John Eberson Official Site
Hosted world premieres of films starring Jimmy Stewart and John Wayne Visit Dallas
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places Wikipedia)
Features an ornate 1,704-seat auditorium with excellent acoustics Tripadvisor
attractions.tips: Parking is available at the Elm Street Garage or nearby surface lots (bring cash). Arrive early to admire the historic lobby architecture and concessions. Use the St. Paul DART station for easy public transit access. ATTPAC, SOVA Hotels
attractions.nearby: Main Street Garden Park, Dallas Arts District, Giant Eyeball, Thanks-Giving Square, Dallas Museum of Art. Tripadvisor, Official Site

attractions.name: The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza The Sixth Floor Museum
attractions.description: The Sixth Floor Museum at Dealey Plaza chronicles the life, death, and legacy of President John F. Kennedy within the historical context of the 1960s. Located in the former Texas School Book Depository, the museum features the infamous sniper's nest and extensive multimedia exhibits. The Sixth Floor Museum, Wikipedia

attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Sunday The Sixth Floor Museum

John F. Kennedy and the Memory of a Nation permanent exhibition The Sixth Floor Museum

attractions.name: Crow Museum of Asian Art of The University of Texas at Dallas (Crow Museum)
attractions.description: The Crow Museum of Asian Art of The University of Texas at Dallas is a premier museum dedicated to celebrating the diverse arts and cultures of Asia, including China, Japan, India, and Korea. Located in the heart of the Dallas Arts District, it offers a serene environment for exploring a rotating calendar of exhibitions that range from ancient artifacts to contemporary masterpieces. The museum also features peaceful outdoor sculpture gardens, providing a tranquil escape for visitors within the urban landscape (Dallas Arts District, Visit Dallas).
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Monday: Closed (Crow Museum)
attractions.admission: Free (Suggested donation: $12 for adults, $7 for seniors; no cash accepted) (Crow Museum)
highlights: Renowned 18th-century Chinese jade collection (Crow Museum)
The Seated Daoist Deity Fountain (AAA)
The Jeffrey Montgomery Collection of Japanese folk art (Crow Museum)
Outdoor sculpture gardens with bamboo, azaleas, and water features (Visit Dallas)
Extensive holdings of Japanese prints and textiles (Yelp)
Rotating contemporary Asian art exhibitions (Crow Museum)
attractions.tips: Visit on weekday mornings for a quieter experience, and note that the museum is cashless for all donations. Public transport via the St. Paul DART Station is a convenient alternative to paid garage parking (Wonderful Museums, Crow Museum).
attractions.nearby: Dallas Museum of Art, Nasher Sculpture Center, Trammell Crow Center, Klyde Warren Park, and the Morton H. Meyerson Symphony Center (Reddit, AAA)

attractions.name: Lockhart Smokehouse
attractions.description: Lockhart Smokehouse brings the legendary flavors of Central Texas barbecue to the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex. Founded by descendants of the iconic Schmidt family of Kreuz Market, the restaurant features meats smoked over Texas post oak and served on butcher paper, staying true to its Lockhart roots. It is one of the only places in the DFW area where visitors can enjoy authentic Kreuz Market sausage.
attractions.address: 400 W Davis St, Dallas, TX 75208, USA
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Free (Order by weight; meats are market priced)
highlights: Authentic Central Texas-style BBQ served on butcher paper
Exclusive DFW destination for world-famous Kreuz Market Sausage
Meats smoked low and slow over real Texas post oak wood
Signature brisket, dry-rubbed spare ribs, and house-brined chicken
Full bar featuring Texas brews and specialty cocktails like the famous Bloody Mary
Weekly daily specials including Beef Ribs on Wednesdays and Burnt Ends on Thursdays
attractions.tips: Arrive early as popular meats often sell out before closing time. The restaurant follows the traditional counter-service model where you order, pay, and pick up your food at the back counter near the smoker. Note that parking in the Bishop Arts District can be challenging, but valet services are often available in the neighborhood. If you're unsure what to order, the staff is happy to offer a 'bite' of the meat before you commit.

attractions.name: Nasher Sculpture Center
attractions.description: The Nasher Sculpture Center is a world-renowned museum in the heart of the Dallas Arts District dedicated to the study, installation, and conservation of modern and contemporary sculpture. It houses the prestigious Raymond and Patsy Nasher Collection within a stunning 55,000-square-foot building designed by architect Renzo Piano and a serene 1.5-acre outdoor garden by Peter Walker. (Nasher Sculpture Center)

highlights: Home to the Raymond and Patsy Nasher Collection, featuring over 300 masterpieces by artists like Picasso, Matisse, and Giacometti (Nasher Sculpture Center)
Architectural landmark designed by Pritzker Prize-winner Renzo Piano featuring a unique barrel-vaulted glass ceiling (Nasher Sculpture Center)
A lush 1.5-acre outdoor sculpture garden designed by landscape architect Peter Walker (Nasher Sculpture Center)
Host of the Nasher Prize, an international award dedicated exclusively to contemporary sculpture (Nasher Sculpture Center)

attractions.name: BrainDead Brewing
attractions.description: BrainDead Brewing was a pioneering craft beer bar and brewpub in Deep Ellum, Dallas, known for its experimental house-made brews and massive wraparound patio (CultureMap Dallas). It served as a neighborhood staple for nearly seven years before permanently closing on November 28, 2021 (Dallas Observer).

Tap wall featuring colorful vintage refrigerator door panels (Deluded Rambling)

attractions.name: Deep Ellum Brewing Company (Official Website)
attractions.description: Deep Ellum Brewing Company was the first craft brewery to open in Dallas in over a decade, establishing itself in 2011 as a pioneer of the city's modern beer scene (Official Website). Known for its bold branding and signature brews like Dallas Blonde and Deep Ellum IPA, the company is deeply influenced by the eclectic and artistic culture of the Deep Ellum neighborhood (Official Website). Although its physical taproom in Dallas closed in May 2024, the brand continues to produce and distribute its beers throughout Texas (D Magazine).
attractions.address: 2823 St Louis St, Dallas, TX 75226, USA (Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Permanently Closed (Taproom and brewhouse shuttered May 30, 2024) (D Magazine)
attractions.admission: Closed (formerly $10–$15 for tours) (From Texas to Beyond)
highlights: First craft brewery to open in Dallas in over a decade (Official Website)
Home to the popular 'Dallas Blonde' Golden Ale and 'Deep Ellum IPA' (Official Website)
Deeply rooted in the artistic and historic Deep Ellum neighborhood of Dallas (Official Website)
Known for a 'reckless' and unapologetic brand identity that celebrates individuality (Official Website)
A key player in the Texas craft beer boom since its founding in 2011 (Craft Beer Austin)
attractions.tips: Since the physical taproom is no longer in operation, look for Deep Ellum's flagship beers like Dallas Blonde and Deep Ellum IPA at local Texas retailers, bars, and restaurants where they remain widely available (D Magazine). Check the official website's 'Beer Finder' tool to locate specific brews near you (Official Website).

attractions.name: Pecan Lodge
attractions.description: Pecan Lodge is a legendary barbecue destination in Dallas known for its time-honored smoking traditions and award-winning meats. Originally started as a stand in the Dallas Farmers Market, it now anchors the Deep Ellum neighborhood with a pit that burns 24 hours a day. The restaurant is celebrated for its brisket, hand-made sausages, and unique southern comfort dishes like the "Hot Mess" sweet potato (Pecan Lodge Official Site).

The "Pitmaster" sandwich featuring brisket, pulled pork, sausage, slaw, and fresh jalapeños (Pecan Lodge Menu)

attractions.name: Sons of Hermann Hall
attractions.description: Sons of Hermann Hall is a historic Deep Ellum venue and Dallas Landmark, recognized as the oldest free-standing wood structure in the city. Built in 1911, it serves as a vibrant community hub hosting live music, weekly swing dancing, and acoustic jams within its storied walls. The venue features an iconic upstairs ballroom and the city's oldest neighborhood bar, preserving a century of Texas fraternal and musical history.

Designated as an official Dallas Landmark in 1987
Home to the city's oldest neighborhood bar with a historic carved wood bar from Germany
Features an iconic upstairs ballroom with its original 100-year-old dance floor

Hosts the 'Electric Campfire Jam,' a long-running open acoustic music circle every Thursday evening
attractions.tips: The venue is not ADA accessible due to its historic architecture. The bar is traditionally cash-only, though an ATM is available on-site. attractions.name: The Bomb Factory
attractions.description: The Bomb Factory is one of Dallas' most storied music venues, originally built in 1914 as a Ford automobile plant before producing munitions during World War II. After a massive $7 million renovation in 2015, the 50,000-square-foot venue became a premier destination for live music in Deep Ellum, hosting iconic artists like Erykah Badu and LCD Soundsystem. Today, it continues to serve as a cornerstone of the city's vibrant cultural and entertainment scene.

attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically includes General Admission, Seated General Admission, and VIP Suites. Tickets can be purchased via AXS or the Box Office on show nights.
highlights: Historic 1914 building once used as a Ford assembly plant and WWII munitions factory.
State-of-the-art sound and lighting systems following a 2015 multi-million dollar renovation.
Flexible venue capacity of up to 4,300 for concerts, corporate events, and private parties.
Located in the heart of the historic Deep Ellum entertainment district.
Features multiple bars and premium mezzanine-level VIP suites for an elevated experience.
attractions.tips: The venue enforces a clear bag policy for all guests. Use DART light rail (Deep Ellum or Baylor University Medical Center stops) to avoid traffic. Parking is available at The Stack garage located directly behind the venue. Re-entry is generally not permitted once you have entered the venue for a show.

attractions.name: Dallas Contemporary
attractions.description: Dallas Contemporary is a non-collecting arts space, or kunsthalle, located in the heart of the Dallas Design District. Its mission is to present the forefront of contemporary artistic practice and production through a rotating schedule of exhibitions, performances, and public programs. By focusing on the art of this moment, the institution fosters the discovery and appreciation of challenging new ideas from regional, national, and international artists. Dallas Contemporary, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Reverchon Park Dallas Parks
attractions.description: Reverchon Park is a historic 41.3-acre community park located in the Oak Lawn area of Dallas, established in 1914 and named after botanist Julien Reverchon. It serves as a nature refuge along Turtle Creek and the Katy Trail, offering a mix of scenic winding paths, historic WPA stonework, and diverse recreational facilities including a baseball stadium, tennis courts, and a large playground. Dallas Parks, The Cultural Landscape Foundation

Originally established as a private park in 1892, it became a public park in 1909 and is often referred to as the city's \"crown jewel.\" The park features winding paths, a scenic creek, and the historic Arlington Hall, serving as a peaceful retreat for residents and visitors alike. (Turtle Creek Conservancy, Wikipedia)

It is one of the most walkable areas in Texas, featuring a rich array of restaurants, nightlife, and green spaces like the Katy Trail. Managed as a Public Improvement District, it blends historic charm with modern urbanist development.

attractions.name: Cotton Bowl Stadium
attractions.description: Cotton Bowl Stadium is a historic outdoor stadium that opened in 1930 and serves as a major venue for college football, including the iconic Red River Rivalry and the State Fair Classic (Wikipedia)). With a capacity of over 91,000, it remains one of the largest and most legendary sporting facilities in the United States, famously hosting the Cotton Bowl Classic for over seven decades before it moved to Arlington (Fair Park Official Site).
attractions.hours: Hours are event-specific; for typical gamedays, parking lots often open 2.5 hours before kickoff, and stadium gates open 60 to 90 minutes before the event start time (Dallas Trinity FC, Vivid Seats). The surrounding Fair Park grounds are generally accessible 24/7 for parking access, though specific attraction hours within the park vary (Parking Systems of America).
attractions.days_open: Open primarily for scheduled events, including Saturdays during the State Fair of Texas (Fair Park Official Site).
attractions.admission: Admission varies by event: Tickets for college football games typically range from $87 to over $500 depending on the matchup and seat tier; Standing Room Only (SRO) tickets can start around $109–$122 for major bowl games (American Arenas, Ticket Club). The stadium is generally closed to the public on non-event days, but the surrounding Fair Park grounds are often free to enter (Fair Park).
attractions.phone: (214) 670-8400 (Fair Park Office), (214) 565-9931 (Stadium Assistance) (Fair Park Official Site)
highlights: Site of the annual Red River Rivalry between the University of Texas and University of Oklahoma (Fair Park Official Site)
Home to the State Fair Classic featuring Grambling State vs. Prairie View A&M (State Fair of Texas)
Original home of the Cotton Bowl Classic from 1937 until 2009 (Fair Park Official Site)
One of the nine host venues for the 1994 FIFA World Cup (Wikipedia))
Historic Art Deco architecture located within the Fair Park National Historic Landmark district (Fair Park Official Site)
Home venue for the Dallas Trinity FC women's soccer team (State Fair of Texas)
attractions.tips: The stadium is a cashless venue, accepting only credit cards or mobile pay for concessions (Dallas Trinity FC). Smoking, including e-cigarettes and vapes, is strictly prohibited inside the stadium, and strollers are not permitted (Fair Park Official Site). To avoid heavy traffic, visitors are encouraged to use the DART Rail Green Line to the Fair Park or MLK, Jr. stations (Dallas Trinity FC).

attractions.name: Dallas Zoo
attractions.description: The Dallas Zoo is the oldest and largest zoological park in Texas, spanning 106 acres and housing over 2,000 animals across more than 400 species. Located just south of downtown, it features award-winning habitats like the Giants of the Savanna and is a leading center for wildlife conservation. (Dallas Zoo Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 650 South R.L. Thornton Freeway, Dallas, TX 75203
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Current Spring Hours: March 2 – July 5). Hours change seasonally to 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM in Summer (starting July 6) and Winter. (Dallas Zoo Hours)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, 364 days a year (Closed on Christmas Day). (Dallas Zoo General Info)
attractions.admission: Variable pricing starting at $18.00. General ranges: Adults (12-64) $18–$25; Youth (3-11) $15–$21; Seniors (65+) $15–$21; Children 2 and under: Free. Members receive free admission and parking. (Dallas Zoo Tickets)
highlights: Giants of the Savanna: An award-winning multispecies habitat where elephants, giraffes, and zebras roam together.
Simmons Hippo Outpost: A 2.1-acre immersive habitat featuring underwater viewing areas for Nile hippos.
Kimberly-Clark Chimpanzee Forest: A lush environment for chimpanzees with climbing structures and artificial termite mounds.
Lacerte Family Children’s Zoo: Features a petting zoo, a nature exchange, and a popular water play stream for kids.
Endangered Species Carousel: A custom-made merry-go-round featuring hand-crafted exotic animal figures.
Monarch Express: A kid-sized electric train that takes families on a scenic trip around Wildflower Ridge.
attractions.tips: Tickets must be reserved online in advance. Bring a change of clothes for children to use at the 'river' in the Children's Zoo. Giraffe feeding is available for an extra fee ($6) and is highly recommended. Outside food and coolers are allowed, though glass, alcohol, and disposable straws are prohibited. (DFWChild, Dallas Zoo FAQ)
attractions.nearby: Bishop Arts District, Klyde Warren Park, Dallas Holocaust and Human Rights Museum, and Marsalis Park. (Tripadvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Fair Park
attractions.category: Cultural & Entertainment Center, Park, National Historic Landmark
attractions.description: Fair Park is a 277-acre recreational, educational, and entertainment complex located in Dallas, Texas. It is a National Historic Landmark home to the world's largest collection of 1930s Art Deco exposition style architecture and serves as the year-round site for museums, performance venues, and the annual State Fair of Texas. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM. (Note: Individual museum and venue hours vary; for example, the Hall of State is open Tue-Sat 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM and Sun 1:00 PM - 5:00 PM). (Fair Park FAQ, Hall of State Hours)
attractions.admission: General admission to the park is free. Individual attractions like the Children's Aquarium Dallas and Texas Discovery Gardens have separate admission fees. Parking is free on non-event days, but costs vary during the State Fair of Texas and other major events. (Fair Park FAQ, Visit Dallas)
highlights: The 700-foot Esplanade reflecting pool and synchronized musical fountain shows. (Official Website)
The historic Hall of State, a premier example of Art Deco architecture in Texas. (Dallas Historical Society)

attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to use the DART Green Line for easy park access to avoid event-day traffic. While the park is dog-friendly (on leash), be aware that many individual buildings and museums do not allow pets inside. Motorized tram tours are often available for those interested in a guided look at the park's unique architecture. (Fair Park Visit, Fair Park FAQ)

It serves as a year-round cultural hub and the permanent home of the annual State Fair of Texas, hosting over 1,200 events annually including concerts, sporting events, and museum exhibits (Fair Park Dallas).

The Fair Park Esplanade, featuring a 700-foot reflecting pool and fountains.

attractions.name: George W. Bush Presidential Library and Museum
attractions.description: The George W. Bush Presidential Center is a nonpartisan institution that houses the 13th Presidential Library, a museum, and the George W. Bush Policy Institute. It chronicles the major events of the 43rd president's two terms in office through interactive exhibits, archival records, and a vast collection of artifacts including gifts from world leaders (Official Site, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday – Saturday: 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m., Sunday: 12:00 p.m. – 5:00 p.m. (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Open daily except Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day (Tour Texas)
attractions.admission: Adult (18-61): $26.00, Senior (62+): $23.00, Youth (13-17): $24.00, Child (5-12): $20.00, Child (0-4): Free, SMU Students/Faculty/Staff: Free, Active-duty military personnel and their families (up to five members): Free (Official Site)
highlights: Replica Oval Office: A scale recreation of the Oval Office decorated exactly as it was during the Bush Administration.

Freedom Hall: A 67-foot high tower featuring a 360-degree LED screen with multimedia clips chronicling American history.
Laura W. Bush Native Texas Park: A 15-acre urban park with native plants, wildflowers, and a one-mile network of trails.
Situation Room: The original White House Situation Room was relocated and reconstructed within the center.
attractions.tips: Tickets must be reserved or purchased in advance. Allow at least 2 hours for a thorough visit; parking is available for a fee in the lot across from the Center. Visitors can also save up to 50% by using a Dallas CityPASS (Official Site, Visit Dallas).

attractions.hours: Visitor Center: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Eugene McDermott Library (Summer Hours): Mon-Thu 7:00 AM – 11:00 PM, Fri 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Sat-Sun 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM (UT Dallas Visitors, McDermott Library)
attractions.admission: Free (Campus Tours and Self-guided visits), Athletic Events: Adults $10, Students $5, Children (3 & under) Free (UT Dallas Athletics)
highlights: Margaret McDermott Trellis Plaza and signature reflecting pools
Four human-scale chess boards honoring the award-winning chess team
The Edith O'Donnell Institute of Art History and the new Athenaeum cultural district
The 'Love Jack' sculpture, a beloved campus landmark by Jim Love
State-of-the-art research facilities like the Natural Science and Engineering Research Laboratory
The Plinth, a central gathering space featuring a granite fountain with a mist column
attractions.tips: Visitors can obtain a complimentary one-day parking pass from the Parking Office (PS3 1.200) with a valid photo ID to park in green-marked spaces. The campus is cashless, so use the Hometown Ticketing app for athletic events. Don't miss the 'Whoosh' salute tradition and the scenic Magnolia-lined Mall. (UT Dallas Visitors)

attractions.name: Trinity Forest Adventure Park & Event Center
attractions.description: Trinity Forest Adventure Park is a self-guided aerial adventure park featuring high ropes courses and zip lines nestled within the hardwoods of the Great Trinity Forest. It offers nine courses of varying difficulty levels, with over 100 obstacles such as wobble bridges, cargo nets, and tight ropes. (Trinity Forest Adventure Park)
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Hours are seasonal and weather-dependent). (Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Adult (16+): $59.95; Youth (10-15): $54.95; Child (6-9): $49.95; Seniors (55+) & Military: $49.95; Littles (4-5): $14.95 (Children's Course); Spectators: Free. Save $5/ticket when booking online. (Trinity Forest Adventure Park)
highlights: 30+ zip lines and 100+ aerial obstacles across 9 courses
Out on a Limb Children's Course for ages 4-7
3-hour self-guided forest exploration experience
Military-style obstacles including cargo nets and wobble bridges
Safety-first environment with harnesses and mandatory Ground School training
Family-owned and operated within one of the largest urban forests in the US
attractions.tips: Wear closed-toe shoes (required; no Crocs). Bring or purchase required gloves. Arrive 15-20 minutes early for check-in and Ground School. Bring water and bug spray. Book online in advance to save $5 per ticket and complete waivers. (Trinity Forest Adventure Park)

attractions.name: Trinity River Greenbelt (Dallas Park and Recreation)
attractions.description: The Trinity River Greenbelt is a massive 3,652.6-acre conservancy park located within the heart of Dallas's Trinity River Corridor (Dallas Park and Recreation). It features a vast network of multi-use trails, nature observation platforms, and boat launches that provide access to the nation's largest urban hardwood forest (Trinity River Corridor Project). The area is part of a major redevelopment project aimed at transforming the floodplain into a premier natural destination for residents and tourists alike (Trinity River Corridor Project).
attractions.address: 3700 Sylvan Avenue, Dallas, TX 75207 (Dallas Park and Recreation)
attractions.region: Trinity River Corridor / West Dallas (Dallas Park and Recreation, Trinity River Corridor Project)
attractions.hours: Monday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Tuesday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Wednesday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Thursday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Friday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Saturday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM; Sunday: 05:00 AM–10:00 PM (Fort Worth Parks, Trinity Trails)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Sunday (Dallas County, Fort Worth Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor, Dallas County)
highlights: 3,652.6-acre conservancy park area (Dallas Park and Recreation)
Multi-use trails for hiking and biking (Dallas Park and Recreation)
Nature observation platforms for wildlife viewing (Dallas Park and Recreation)
Canoe and kayak boat launches (Trinity River Corridor Project)
Part of the Great Trinity Forest, the largest urban hardwood forest in the nation (Trinity River Corridor Project)
Historic Lock and Dam Number 4 located within the Riverbend Preserve (DFW Urban Wildlife)
attractions.tips: Visit the Trinity River Audubon Center for educational exhibits and guided nature walks (Audubon). Check weather conditions before visiting, as the area is a floodplain and trails may flood during heavy rains (Reddit/Dallas). Wear a headlamp if jogging outside daylight hours, as many trail sections are unlit (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Trinity River Audubon Center, Harold Simmons Park (under development), Ron Kirk Bridge, Trinity Groves, and downtown Dallas landmarks (Trinity River Corridor Project)

attractions.name: White Rock Lake Park
attractions.description: White Rock Lake is a 1,015-acre city lake located approximately 5 miles northeast of downtown Dallas. Known as the "jewel in the crown" of the Dallas Park System, it is a premier destination for outdoor recreation, featuring scenic views, extensive trail networks, and diverse wildlife habitats in an urban setting.
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM Daily
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 9.33-mile hike and bike trail loop around the lake
Dallas Arboretum and Botanical Garden on the southeastern shore
Bath House Cultural Center and White Rock Lake Museum
Audubon Society bird-watching areas and designated wetlands
Kayaking, canoeing, and stand-up paddleboarding concessions
Multiple fishing piers and sailing clubs (Corinthian and White Rock Boat Club)
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends and holidays as parking lots fill up quickly. Be mindful on the trails as they are shared between cyclists, runners, and walkers. Note that swimming has been prohibited since 1952, and motorized boats are restricted to 10.5 horsepower or less.
attractions.nearby: Dallas Arboretum & Botanical Gardens, Bath House Cultural Center, Winfrey Point, Santa Fe Trail, White Rock Dog Park

attractions.name: Zero Gravity Thrill Amusement Park Wikipedia
attractions.description: Zero Gravity Thrill Amusement Park was a one-of-a-kind "extreme ride amusement center" that operated in Dallas from 1992 to 2021. It specialized exclusively in high-intensity attractions designed to simulate freefall, weightlessness, and extreme G-forces for adrenaline seekers. Wikipedia, Explorow
attractions.hours: Mon-Thu 4:00 PM – 10:00 PM, Fri 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM (midnight), Sat 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sun 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM Holidify, Viator

Nothin' But Net: A 16-story freefall into a stunt-quality safety net with no cords Yonder Tours

attractions.tips: Hold your head firmly against the headrest during the Texas Blastoff to avoid jolting. Empty all pockets and use provided lockers before the Bungee Jump.
